presets.name: driving-kit
presets.description: Punchy house kit: shaped kick, bright hats, glued with parallel compression and light drive (Night Shift v3 drums)

presets.name: 808-trap-kit
presets.description: 808/trap kit: deep, slow-glide kick with a long boomy tail, tight hat-roll-ready closed hat, saturated low end (research: TR-808 lineage, hip-hop/trap)

presets.name: techno-kit
presets.description: Techno kit: tight snapping 909-style kick, mechanical scooped-mid snare, driving bright 16th-note hats, heavily glued (research: TR-909 lineage, dark/mechanical techno character)

presets.name: boom-bap-kit
presets.description: Boom-bap kit: crunchy dirty kick, fat tonal snare with real body, dampened 'vinyl' top end, sampled-and-worn character (research: 90s hip-hop convention, midrange weight over sub/air)

presets.name: lofi-kit
presets.description: Lo-fi kit: soft muted kick, muffled dark hats, heavy bitcrush grit under a hard lowpass, gentle room send (research: lo-fi hip-hop convention — bitcrush + aggressive high-cut over sharp transients)

presets.name: acoustic-rock-kit
presets.description: Acoustic/rock kit: higher-pitched natural kick with a tight beater click, a real snare shell's tonal ring, bright open hats, clean and unfiltered with light room ambience — the least processed kit in the library

presets.name: deep-sub-bass
presets.description: Sub-anchored bass with a detuned square layer, plucky filter envelope, and glide; add duckSource yourself for sidechain (Night Shift v3 bass)

presets.name: sub-sine-bass
presets.description: Bass — clean, flat sustained sine sub with almost no harmonic content and no filter movement; the opposite design of deep-sub-bass's plucky moving saw/square hybrid, for parts that need pure low-end weight

presets.name: reese-bass
presets.description: Bass — thick detuned dual-saw Reese with wide unison and a slow breathing filter LFO; the beating/chorusing interaction between layers is the point (research: detuned saws, Kevin Saunderson lineage, D&B/dubstep)

presets.name: wobble-bass
presets.description: Bass — dubstep wobble: fast deep filter-cutoff LFO over a resonant square, an FM growl layer underneath, digital grit on top (research: rhythmic LFO-on-cutoff is the defining wobble mechanism)

presets.name: acid-bass
presets.description: Bass — TB-303-style acid: very high filter resonance driven hard by the filter envelope, short glide between notes for slides, the 'squelch' comes entirely from envelope-into-resonance

presets.name: fm-bass
presets.description: Bass — DX-style FM bass: the tone comes from a hard FM modulator (not the filter, which stays open), giving a bite that cuts through a dense mix without eating headroom

presets.name: bright-lead
presets.description: Cutting unison lead with a detuned second saw, plucky filter envelope, and echoing delay/reverb sends (Night Shift v3 lead)

presets.name: supersaw-lead
presets.description: Lead — huge 7-voice unison supersaw stack, wide, sustained; anthemic trance/EDM lead character rather than bright-lead's tighter 3-voice pluck-envelope stab

presets.name: pluck-lead
presets.description: Lead — staccato plucky lead for riffs: short amp envelope, fast filter-envelope snap opening into a low sustain, distinguishing a percussive lead from a sustained one

presets.name: square-chip-lead
presets.description: Lead — thin retro chiptune/8-bit lead: plain square wave, no unison, no filtering to speak of, heavy bitcrush for the digital low-bit-depth character

presets.name: fm-bell-lead
presets.description: Lead — bell-like FM lead: a non-integer FM ratio (3.5) makes the overtones inharmonic, the signature 'bell' timbre distinct from subtractive leads built from filtered saws/squares

presets.name: lush-pad
presets.description: Wide unison saw pad: slow attack, dual detuned oscillators, LFO filter motion, deep reverb/delay sends (Night Shift v3 pad)

presets.name: warm-pad
presets.description: Pad — mellow, dark analog pad: triangle-based instead of lush-pad's saw stack, lower cutoff, subtler detune and LFO depth for a rounder, less bright texture

presets.name: string-pad
presets.description: Pad — bowed-strings emulation: a slower bow-like attack than lush-pad, 4-voice ensemble detune for a section-of-strings beating, brighter and more mid-forward than warm-pad

presets.name: glass-pad
presets.description: Pad — bright shimmering FM pad: FM (not filtered subtractive) provides the tone, a slow tremolo LFO on amplitude gives the 'shimmer', long reverb/delay tail

presets.name: dark-pad
presets.description: Pad — moody, filtered ambient pad: low cutoff with a slow filter-envelope opening (not an LFO sweep) so the pad gradually brightens over the course of a held note, high resonance for tension

presets.name: crystal-pluck
presets.description: Pluck — bright plucky bell tone: near-zero amp attack/sustain (research: pluck = short attack + short decay + low sustain) with a fast filter-envelope snap that opens bright then retreats dark

presets.name: warm-pluck
presets.description: Pluck — softer, rounder pluck than crystal-pluck: darker starting cutoff, gentler filter-envelope amount, a sub sine layer for body instead of brightness

presets.name: fm-pluck
presets.description: Pluck — metallic electric-piano-ish pluck built entirely from FM rather than a filter envelope; the shared amp envelope's fast decay is what gives it a plucked shape

presets.name: marimba-pluck
presets.description: Pluck — woody mallet-percussion character: sine fundamental plus a detuned triangle partial for a slightly inharmonic 'wooden bar' ring, faster decay and lower sustain than the other plucks

presets.name: e-piano
presets.description: Keys — FM electric piano: a moderate FM modulation index gives the classic 'tine' bell-attack-into-warm-body EP timbre, light chorus send

presets.name: bell-keys
presets.description: Keys — bright FM bell keys: higher FM modulation index and a non-integer ratio (3.5, same inharmonic-overtone technique as fm-bell-lead) than e-piano, longer decay, more reverb

presets.name: organ-keys
presets.description: Keys — drawbar-style organ: a near-instant on/off amp envelope (no decay, full sustain — organs don't decay while held) and an octave+fifth second-oscillator layer standing in for drawbar harmonics, chorus send for a Leslie-ish width

presets.name: warm-keys
presets.description: Keys — subtractive (non-FM) warm electric keys: filtered triangle with a gentle filter-envelope swell instead of e-piano's FM bite, for a mellower Wurlitzer-ish character

presets.name: arp-pluck
presets.description: Arp — short percussive voicing for fast note runs: tighter decay than any Pluck-category preset, heavy delay send so a fast arpeggiated pattern builds its own rhythmic echo trail

presets.name: arp-bell
presets.description: Arp — FM bell voicing for melodic trance/EDM-style arps: short-medium decay so a 16th-note run stays articulate, delay-forward for the classic 'gated bell arp' effect

presets.name: arp-sequence
presets.description: Arp — retro sequencer voicing: resonant square wave with a fast cutoff LFO so a repeating note pattern gets subtle per-cycle timbral movement rather than sounding static

presets.name: riser-sweep
presets.description: FX — noise riser for transition builds: dominant noise oscillator layer under a long (2s) filter-envelope attack sweeping the cutoff up, long amp envelope to match

presets.name: noise-impact
presets.description: FX — broadband noise impact/hit: full-level noise oscillator, short decay, distorted for extra weight, big reverb return so it reads as a one-shot boom rather than a sustained texture

presets.name: drone-texture
presets.description: FX — evolving ambient drone: wide 5-voice unison saw, two independent slow LFOs (one on cutoff, one on amplitude) so the texture never quite repeats, very long attack/release
